content.title: The phrase 你觉得怎么样？
content.body:
**你觉得怎么样？** (Nǐ juéde zěnmeyàng?) is a standard way to ask someone's opinion. It literally means "You think how?" but translates to "What do you think?" or "How do you find it?"

**Components:**
- 你 (nǐ) — you
- 觉得 (juéde) — think/feel
- 怎么样 (zěnmeyàng) — how/how about

This question is extremely ve

content.body:

content.title: Describing visual impressions
content.body:
When giving opinions about appearance or visual appeal (people, clothes, movies, scenery), use 觉得 or 认为 combined with 好看 or its negative.

**Examples:**
- 我觉得很好看。(Wǒ juéde hěn hǎokàn.) — I think it's very good-looking/attractive.
- 你觉得这个电影好看吗？(Nǐ juéde zhège diànyǐng hǎokàn ma?) — Do you think this

content.title: The adjective 好听 (hǎotīng) — good-sounding
content.body:
**好听** (hǎotīng) means "good-sounding," "pleasant to listen to," or "nice-sounding." It breaks down as 好 (hǎo, good) + 听 (tīng, to listen/to hear) = "good to listen to."

**Examples:**
- 很好听。(Hěn hǎotīng.) — Very pleasant to listen to./Sounds very nice.
- 不好听。(Bù hǎotīng.) — Doesn't sound good./Unpl

content.title: Using 不错 in opinion statements
content.body:
不错 (pretty good/not bad) is frequently used with 觉得 and 认为 to give moderate positive assessments. It's polite and commonly used in Chinese conversation.

**Examples:**
- 我觉得不错。(Wǒ juéde bú cuò.) — I think it's pretty good.
- 你认为不错吗？(Nǐ rènwéi bú cuò ma?) — Do you think it's not bad?
- 这个我觉得不错。(Zhège

content.title: Asking for opinions with 你喜欢吗？
content.body:
**你喜欢吗？** (Nǐ xǐhuan ma?) means "Do you like it?" This is a direct yes/no question asking about someone's preference or liking.

**Note:** 喜欢 (xǐhuan, to like) was taught in subtopic 6.3. If you need to review this vocabulary, refer back to that section.

**Structure:**
你 (nǐ, you) + 喜欢 (xǐhuan, to

content.title: The adjective 好吃 (hǎochī) — delicious
content.body:
**好吃** (hǎochī) means "delicious" or "tasty." It literally breaks down as 好 (hǎo, good) + 吃 (chī, to eat) = "good to eat."

**Examples:**
- 很好吃。(Hěn hǎochī.) — Very delicious.
- 不好吃。(Bù hǎochī.) — Not delicious./Not tasty.
- 这个很好吃。(Zhège hěn hǎochī.) — This is very delicious.

好吃 is one of the most

content.title: Responding to opinion questions
content.body:
When someone asks for your opinion, your response should match the question type and use appropriate structures.

**Response patterns:**

For 你觉得怎么样？:
- 我觉得 + [assessment]
- Examples: 很好 / 不错 / 很好吃 / 很好看 / 一般

For 你喜欢吗？:
- 喜欢 / 不喜欢
- Can add intensity: 很喜欢 / 不太喜欢

For 你觉得...吗？ (Do you think it's...?

content.title: Basic structure: Subject + 觉得 + adjective phrase
content.body:
When expressing opinions with 觉得, the basic sentence structure places the opinion holder as the subject, followed by 觉得, then the opinion itself (usually an adjective or adjective phrase).

**Structure:**
Subject + 觉得 + [adjective/adjective phrase/clause]

**Examples:**
- 我觉得很好。(Wǒ juéde hěn hǎo.) —

content.title: The pattern 好 + verb compounds
content.body:
The character **好** (hǎo, good) can combine with verbs to create compound adjectives that mean "good to [verb]" or "easy to [verb]."

**Common examples:**
- 好吃 (hǎochī) — good to eat = delicious
- 好看 (hǎokàn) — good to look at = good-looking/attractive
- 好听 (hǎotīng) — good to listen to = good-sound

content.title: Giving and receiving compliments
content.body:
In Chinese culture, compliments often use 觉得 + adjective structures, and understanding how to give and graciously receive them is important.

**Note:** Some of the structures below use 得 (complement) and 的 (modification) patterns that are beyond HSK 1 level. These are presented as reference material

content.title: Restaurant conversation scenario
content.body:
**Scenario:** You're at a Chinese restaurant with a friend trying different dishes.

**Vocabulary note:** This scenario uses several new words introduced in this integration context:
- 菜 (cài, HSK 2) — dish/food item
- 好喝 (hǎohē, HSK 2) — good-tasting (for drinks)
- 饭店 (fàndiàn, HSK 2) — restaurant

content.title: Expressing opinions about food
content.body:
When discussing food preferences in Chinese, you combine opinion structures with food-specific adjectives. The most natural flow is to use 觉得 or 认为 with 好吃 or its negative.

**Common expressions:**
- 我觉得很好吃。(Wǒ juéde hěn hǎochī.) — I think it's very delicious.
- 你觉得好吃吗？(Nǐ juéde hǎochī ma?) — Do you

content.title: Expressing "I think" with 觉得 (juéde)
content.body:
The verb **觉得** (juéde) means "to think" or "to feel" in the sense of expressing an opinion. It's one of the most common ways to share your thoughts or impressions about something.

**Structure:**
Subject + 觉得 + opinion/statement

**Examples:**
- 我觉得很好。(Wǒ juéde hěn hǎo.) — I think it's very good.
-

content.title: Movie discussion
content.body:
You and your Chinese friend just finished watching a movie. Practice expressing and exchanging opinions about it.

**Useful vocabulary for this context:**
- 电影 (diànyǐng, HSK 2) — movie
- 觉得 (juéde) — to think/feel
- 怎么样 (zěnmeyàng) — how/how about
- 好看 (hǎokàn) — good (for movies)
- 喜欢 (xǐhuan) — t

content.title: Comparing 你觉得怎么样？ and 你喜欢吗？
content.body:
These two questions ask for different types of feedback. Understanding when to use each makes your Chinese more natural.

**你觉得怎么样？** (What do you think?)
- Asks for general opinion/assessment
- Open-ended: allows various responses
- More objective-sounding
- Response: 我觉得 + opinion

**你喜欢吗？** (Do y

content.title: Talking about music and sounds
content.body:
When expressing opinions about auditory experiences (music, songs, voices, sounds), combine opinion structures with 好听.

**Examples:**
- 我觉得很好听。(Wǒ juéde hěn hǎotīng.) — I think it sounds very nice.
- 你觉得这个音乐好听吗？(Nǐ juéde zhège yīnyuè hǎotīng ma?) — Do you think this music sounds good?
- 我认为不太好听。(Wǒ

content.title: Shopping opinions
content.body:
When shopping with Chinese friends or salespeople, you'll often be asked for your opinions about items. This integrates preference expressions with practical contexts.

**Vocabulary note:** This section uses HSK 2 vocabulary:
- 件 (jiàn, HSK 2) — measure word for clothing
- 衣服 (yīfu, HSK 2) — clothin

content.title: The adjective 好看 (hǎokàn) — good-looking
content.body:
**好看** (hǎokàn) means "good-looking," "attractive," or "nice to look at." It breaks down as 好 (hǎo, good) + 看 (kàn, to look/to see) = "good to look at."

**Examples:**
- 很好看。(Hěn hǎokàn.) — Very good-looking./Very attractive.
- 不好看。(Bù hǎokàn.) — Not good-looking./Not attractive.
- 这个电影很好看。(Zhège di

How would you compliment someone's cooking?
